schema.description: A server for managing Google Spreadsheets, including creating, reading, and modifying spreadsheets, sheets, rows, columns, and cell data.

tools.name: list_spreadsheets
tools.description: List all spreadsheets in the configured Google Drive folder.

spreadsheets.description: List of spreadsheets available in the configured Google Drive folder.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet, as found in the spreadsheet URL.

title.description: The display title of the spreadsheet.

tools.name: get_spreadsheet_info
tools.description: Get basic information about a Google Spreadsheet, including its title, sheets, and timestamps.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet, as found in the spreadsheet URL.
The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet, as found in the spreadsheet URL.

title.description: The display title of the spreadsheet.

sheets.description: List of sheet names contained within the spreadsheet.

created_time.description: The timestamp when the spreadsheet was created.

modified_time.description: The timestamp when the spreadsheet was last modified.

tools.name: list_sheets
tools.description: List all sheet tabs in a Google Spreadsheet.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet, as found in the spreadsheet URL.

sheet_names.description: List of sheet tab names within the spreadsheet.

tools.name: get_sheet_data
tools.description: Get cell data from a specific sheet in a Google Spreadsheet, optionally limited to a specific range.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet, as found in the spreadsheet URL.

sheet.description: The name of the sheet tab to read data from.

range.description: Cell range in A1 notation (e.g., 'A1:C10'). If omitted, returns all data in the sheet.

data.description: 2D array representing the sheet data, where each inner array is a row of cell values.

tools.name: create_spreadsheet
tools.description: Create a new Google Spreadsheet with the specified title.

title.description: The display title for the new spreadsheet.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the newly created Google Spreadsheet.

title.description: The display title of the newly created spreadsheet.

url.description: The URL to access the newly created spreadsheet in Google Sheets.

tools.name: create_sheet
tools.description: Create a new sheet tab in an existing Google Spreadsheet.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet, as found in the spreadsheet URL.

title.description: The display title for the new sheet tab.

sheet_id.description: The unique numeric identifier of the newly created sheet tab within the spreadsheet.

title.description: The display title of the newly created sheet tab.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet containing the new sheet.

tools.name: batch_update_cells
tools.description: Update cells in a Google Spreadsheet. Supports updating a single range or multiple ranges in batch.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet, as found in the spreadsheet URL.

sheet.description: The name of the sheet tab to update.

ranges.description: Dictionary mapping range strings (in A1 notation) to 2D arrays of values for batch updates. Use this for updating multiple ranges at once.

range.description: Single cell range in A1 notation (e.g., 'A1:C10') for a single update. Use with 'data' parameter.

data.description: 2D array of values to write to the specified single range. Required when 'range' parameter is provided.

updated_ranges.description: List of cell ranges that were successfully updated.

total_updated_cells.description: The total number of cells that were updated across all ranges.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet that was updated.

tools.name: add_rows
tools.description: Add new rows to the end of a sheet in a Google Spreadsheet.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet, as found in the spreadsheet URL.

sheet.description: The name of the sheet tab to add rows to.

count.description: Number of rows to add. Defaults to 1 if not specified.

added_rows.description: The number of rows that were successfully added.

sheet.description: The name of the sheet tab where rows were added.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet that was modified.

tools.name: add_columns
tools.description: Add new columns to a sheet in a Google Spreadsheet.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet, as found in the spreadsheet URL.

sheet.description: The name of the sheet tab to add columns to.

count.description: Number of columns to add. Defaults to 1 if not specified.

added_columns.description: The number of columns that were successfully added.

sheet.description: The name of the sheet tab where columns were added.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet that was modified.

tools.name: delete_rows
tools.description: Delete rows from a sheet in a Google Spreadsheet starting at a specified index.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet, as found in the spreadsheet URL.

sheet.description: The name of the sheet tab to delete rows from.

start_index.description: The 0-based index of the first row to delete.

count.description: Number of consecutive rows to delete starting from start_index. Defaults to 1 if not specified.

deleted_rows.description: The number of rows that were successfully deleted.

sheet.description: The name of the sheet tab where rows were deleted.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet that was modified.

tools.name: delete_columns
tools.description: Delete columns from a sheet in a Google Spreadsheet starting at a specified index.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet, as found in the spreadsheet URL.

sheet.description: The name of the sheet tab to delete columns from.

start_index.description: The 0-based index of the first column to delete.

count.description: Number of consecutive columns to delete starting from start_index. Defaults to 1 if not specified.

deleted_columns.description: The number of columns that were successfully deleted.

sheet.description: The name of the sheet tab where columns were deleted.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet that was modified.

tools.name: copy_sheet
tools.description: Copy an existing sheet tab within a Google Spreadsheet to create a duplicate with a new name.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet, as found in the spreadsheet URL.

sheet.description: The name of the sheet tab to copy.

new_sheet_name.description: The display title for the new copied sheet tab.

sheet_id.description: The unique numeric identifier of the newly created copied sheet tab.

title.description: The display title of the newly created copied sheet tab.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet containing the copied sheet.

tools.name: rename_sheet
tools.description: Rename an existing sheet tab in a Google Spreadsheet.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet, as found in the spreadsheet URL.

sheet.description: The current name of the sheet tab to rename.

new_name.description: The new display title for the sheet tab.

old_name.description: The previous name of the sheet tab before renaming.

new_name.description: The new name of the sheet tab after renaming.

spreadsheet_id.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet containing the renamed sheet.

scenarios.description: Basic scenario with one spreadsheet containing a single sheet with sample data
Multiple spreadsheets with various sheet configurations and mixed data types
Comprehensive scenario with multiple spreadsheets, extensive sheets, and complex data relationships
Edge case scenario with empty collections, extreme values, and special characters

Long Text Test: This is a normal length text field

This is an extremely long text that could potentially cause issues in some systems if not handled properly. It contains multiple sentences and goes on for quite a while to test how the system handles very long string values in spreadsheet cells.

errors.error_details: Tool rejected valid start_index values (3, 4) with 'Invalid start_index' error
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Successful deletion of rows. Actual: Error stating 'Invalid start_index' for valid indices
errors.root_cause: The delete_rows tool appears to have incorrect validation logic that rejects valid row indices

tool_executions.error: Invalid start_index errors for valid indices

errors.error_details: GoogleSheets-get_spreadsheet_info fail before execution: Error executing tool get_spreadsheet_info: Spreadsheet invalid_id not found
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected validation error for invalid spreadsheet ID, got proper error message
errors.root_cause: Tool correctly validates spreadsheet ID and returns appropriate error for non-existent spreadsheet

errors.error_details: GoogleSheets-delete_rows fail before execution: Error executing tool delete_rows: start_index must be non-negative
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected validation error for negative start_index, got proper error message
errors.root_cause: Tool correctly validates input parameters and returns appropriate error for invalid start_index

errors.error_details: GoogleSheets-delete_rows fail before execution: Error executing tool delete_rows: Invalid start_index 4
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected validation error for out-of-bounds start_index, got proper error message
errors.root_cause: Tool correctly validates that start_index is within valid range for the sheet

errors.error_details: Tool rejected valid start_index values (3, 4) with 'Invalid start_index' error
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Successful deletion of rows. Actual: Error stating 'Invalid start_index' for valid indices
errors.root_cause: The delete_rows tool appears to have incorrect validation logic that rejects valid row indices

errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected validation error for invalid spreadsheet ID, got proper error message
errors.root_cause: Tool correctly validates spreadsheet ID and returns appropriate error for non-existent spreadsheet

errors.root_cause: Tool correctly validates input parameters and returns appropriate error for invalid start_index

errors.root_cause: Tool correctly validates that start_index is within valid range for the sheet
